title: Generate a balanced MaxDiff / best-worst experimental design
summary: Generate a balanced MaxDiff / best-worst experimental design using simulated annealing. Optimizes item-balance and pair-balance across tasks and versions and writes design CSVs (wide and long), summary statistics, and exports to run_dir.
description: Constructs a multi-version MaxDiff (best-worst) experimental design from a small set of structural inputs (`n_items`, `items_per_task`, `tasks_per_version`, `versions`) by minimising an item-balance and pair-balance objective with simulated annealing. The optimiser is item-label agnostic; labels and forbidden item pairs are layered on top of the integer search space and used only for export and feasibility filtering. Optionally runs `n_starts` independent search chains in parallel and keeps the lowest-scoring design.
usage:
[["design-maxdiff::design"]]
n_items = <int>
items_per_task = <int>
tasks_per_version = <int>
versions = <int>
run_dir = "<path>"
run_name = "<name>"
# optional:
seed = <int>
objective = "sequential" | "combined" | "within"
engine = "sa" | "greedy"
iterations = <int>
initial_temperature = <number>
cooling_rate = <number>
exploration_rate = <number>
n_starts = <int>
threads = <int>
item_labels = ["a", "b", ...]
forbidden_pairs = [[1, 2], [3, 7], ...]

arguments.name: n_items
arguments.description: Total number of items in the study (the size of the item universe). Must be >= `items_per_task`. The optimiser works on integer item indices in the range 1..=n_items; human-readable names live in `item_labels`.

arguments.name: items_per_task
arguments.description: Number of items shown to a respondent in a single task (the 'A' dimension of an A-out-of-N design). Must be >= 2 and <= `n_items`. Larger values produce richer pairwise information per task but more cognitive load on the respondent.

arguments.name: tasks_per_version
arguments.description: Number of tasks each respondent sees within a single design version. Together with `n_items` and `items_per_task` this determines the achievable per-version exposure: total slot count is `tasks_per_version * items_per_task`.

arguments.name: versions
arguments.description: Number of design versions (questionnaire forms) to generate. With multiple versions, balance is enforced both within each version and across the global pool of all versions, so that no item or item-pair is systematically over- or under-exposed.

arguments.name: run_dir
arguments.description: Output directory. Created if it does not exist. The plugin writes the wide and long design CSVs, three summary CSVs, and machine-readable `design.json` / `summary.json` here. See the `Value` section for the full file list.

arguments.name: run_name
arguments.description: Short identifier for this run. Non-empty string. Used to label the run directory and to tag output artifacts so multiple designs written under the same `run_dir` do not collide.

arguments.name: seed
arguments.description: Random seed for the simulated annealing chain. Provide an explicit value for reproducibility; omit (or pass null) for a non-deterministic seed. Note that with `n_starts > 1`, distinct per-chain seeds are derived from this base seed.

arguments.name: objective
arguments.description:
Selects which composite objective the optimiser minimises.

`"sequential"` (default) optimises versions one at a time, each with its own full SA chain. The iteration budget applies independently to each version, so total SA work = `iterations × versions`. Global cross-version balance terms guide each version toward filling gaps left by earlier ones. Use this for virtually all studies.

`"within"` is identical to `sequential` in algorithm and SA settings but with the cross-version balance terms disabled. Each version is optimised in complete isolation. Use this when versions will be fielded independently or when cross-version balance is explicitly not required. Expect slightly worse aggregate pair coverage across versions compared to `sequential`, but identical within-version balance.

`"combined"` treats all versions as a single object optimised by one SA chain. The total iteration budget is shared across all versions (~`iterations / versions` steps per version on average). Critically, the SA temperature cools continuously for the entire run — with the default `cooling_rate = 0.9995` the chain freezes after roughly 60,000 steps, leaving essentially no exploration for large version counts. Only practical for `versions <= ~15` with correctly scaled `iterations` and `cooling_rate` (see Notes). Even then, `sequential` usually matches or exceeds its quality at a fraction of the runtime.

arguments.name: engine
arguments.description: Optimisation engine. `"sa"` is the simulated annealing solver (production default). `"greedy"` is a deterministic baseline used mainly as a sanity check or for very small problems where random restart isn't needed.

arguments.name: iterations
arguments.description: SA iteration budget. Meaning depends on `objective`: for `sequential` and `within` the budget applies independently to each version (total SA work = `iterations × versions`); for `combined` it is the total budget shared across all versions (~`iterations / versions` steps per version average). Default: 300,000. For `combined`, set `iterations = versions × 300000` to match `sequential`'s per-version exploration. For large studies, increasing `n_starts` (with matched `threads`) is usually more effective than increasing `iterations` on a single chain.

arguments.name: exploration_rate
arguments.description: Probability that a single SA proposal is a `replace` move (swap one item for another from outside the task) rather than a `swap` move (exchange two items already in the task). Number in (0, 1]. Lower values are more conservative; the default 0.65 is a good general-purpose setting.

arguments.name: initial_temperature
arguments.description: Starting temperature for the SA acceptance rule. Higher values accept more uphill moves early in the search. Number > 0. Default: 10.0. For `sequential` and `within`, the temperature restarts fresh at `initial_temperature` at the beginning of each version. For `combined`, it starts once and cools continuously for the entire run — see `cooling_rate` for how to scale correctly.

arguments.name: cooling_rate
arguments.description: Per-iteration multiplicative cooling factor (`T ← T × cooling_rate`). Must be in (0, 1). With the default `initial_temperature = 10.0` and `cooling_rate = 0.9995`, the chain reaches near-zero temperature after roughly 60,000 steps — acceptable for `sequential` (temperature restarts fresh each version) but fatal for `combined` with large version counts. For `combined`, slow the cooling so the chain stays warm for the entire run. The target rate to keep T above 1.0 for the full budget is approximately `cooling_rate ≈ 1 - 2.3 / (versions × iterations)`. Recommended `combined` settings by version count: 5 versions → iterations=1500000, cooling_rate=0.9999985; 10 versions → iterations=3000000, cooling_rate=0.9999992; 15 versions → iterations=4500000, cooling_rate=0.9999995. Default: 0.9995.

arguments.name: verbose
arguments.description: When `true`, the plugin emits one `event.run.progress` envelope per completed version, carrying the version number, best score, and accepted-move count. Total envelope volume scales with `versions` only and is safe for any `iterations` value.

arguments.name: print_every
arguments.description: Cadence (in SA iterations) for `bw-core`'s internal verbose-print path. NOTE: in plugin/protocol mode this knob has no effect on the wire stream — protocol output is per-version, not per-iteration. Retained for the standalone CLI's `--verbose` mode.

arguments.name: n_starts
arguments.description: Number of independent SA restarts; the lowest-scoring design across all chains is kept. Integer >= 1. Setting `n_starts > 1` enables multi-start search and benefits from `threads`. A common pattern is `n_starts = 4–8` with `threads` matched to physical cores.

arguments.name: threads
arguments.description: Worker threads used for parallel restarts. Integer >= 1. Only meaningful when `n_starts > 1`. Higher values are linearly faster up to the number of physical cores.

arguments.name: proposal_batch_size
arguments.description: Batch size for the batched-proposals search mode (advanced). Integer >= 1. Ignored unless the search mode resolves to `batched_proposals`; most users should leave this at the default.

arguments.name: weight_item_balance
arguments.description: Sequential / within objective: weight on per-version item-exposure balance. Number >= 0. Increase to penalise designs where some items appear noticeably more often than others within a version.

arguments.name: weight_pair_balance
arguments.description: Sequential / within objective: weight on per-version item-pair co-occurrence balance. Number >= 0. Increase to penalise designs where some pairs of items rarely (or never) appear together in a task.

arguments.name: weight_global_item_balance
arguments.description: Sequential objective only: weight on running global item balance across versions completed so far. Number >= 0. This term is what makes `sequential` produce balanced designs across the pool, and is what `within` drops.

arguments.name: weight_global_pair_balance
arguments.description: Sequential objective only: weight on running global pair balance across versions completed so far. Number >= 0.

arguments.name: weight_version_item_balance
arguments.description: Combined objective only: weight on per-version item balance. Number >= 0. Used by the joint optimiser when `objective = "combined"`.

arguments.name: weight_version_pair_balance
arguments.description: Combined objective only: weight on per-version pair balance. Number >= 0.

arguments.name: weight_total_item_balance
arguments.description: Combined objective only: weight on total item balance across all versions. Number >= 0.

arguments.name: weight_total_pair_balance
arguments.description: Combined objective only: weight on total pair balance across all versions. Number >= 0.

arguments.name: item_labels
arguments.description: Optional human-readable labels for items. Length must equal `n_items` if provided. Used in CSV/JSON exports only — the optimiser is label-agnostic and operates purely on integer item indices.

arguments.name: forbidden_pairs
arguments.description: Pairs of 1-based item indices that must never co-occur in the same task. Each entry is a 2-element integer array `[a, b]` with both values in `1..=n_items`. The optimiser treats any task containing such a pair as infeasible and refuses moves that would create one.

details:
The optimiser searches the space of `(versions, tasks_per_version)` blocks of `items_per_task` integer item indices, minimising a weighted sum of imbalance terms. The two basic terms are item-frequency variance (every item should appear roughly the same number of times) and item-pair co-occurrence variance (every unordered pair of items should appear together roughly the same number of times). With multiple versions, the optimiser tracks both per-version balance and global (across-version) balance, so that respondents who see different forms still collectively see a balanced item pool.

The `objective` argument switches between three search strategies. `"sequential"` (default) builds one version at a time, running a complete SA chain for each version with the temperature resetting to `initial_temperature` at the start of every version. Global cross-version balance terms steer each version toward compensating for imbalances accumulated in prior versions. This is fast, scales to any version count, and produces excellent quality — use it for virtually all studies. `"within"` is identical to `"sequential"` in algorithm and SA tuning but with all cross-version balance terms zeroed out; versions are optimised in complete isolation. `"combined"` jointly optimises all versions using a single SA chain — the full `iterations` budget is shared across all versions (~`iterations / versions` steps per version on average).

Why `"combined"` underperforms for large version counts: the SA temperature cools continuously for the entire run. With the default `cooling_rate = 0.9995` and `initial_temperature = 10.0`, the chain reaches near-zero temperature after roughly 60,000 steps regardless of the total `iterations`. With 100 versions this leaves around 600 warm steps per version on average — effectively random search with no exploitation. To use `"combined"` correctly you must scale `iterations = versions × 300000` and slow `cooling_rate` using the formula `cooling_rate ≈ 1 - 2.3 / (versions × iterations)`. Even with correct scaling, `"combined"` rarely produces better designs than `"sequential"` and takes proportionally longer. Its only theoretical advantage is joint global pair-balance optimisation — a distinction that matters only for very small studies (V ≤ ~5) where the sequential accumulation of global balance converges slowly.

Multi-start search via `n_starts > 1` runs independent SA chains from different random seeds and keeps the best result. Combined with `threads`, this is the most reliable way to improve solution quality on hard problems — typically more effective than increasing `iterations` on a single chain.

Forbidden pairs are enforced as a hard feasibility constraint: any proposal that would put a forbidden pair into the same task is rejected before scoring. If feasibility cannot be satisfied (e.g., the forbidden-pair graph leaves too few items per task), the optimiser will fail at startup with a validation error.
value:
On success, `run_dir` contains:

- `design_wide.csv` — wide-format design matrix with one row per (version, task) and one column per item slot.
- `design_long.csv` — long-format design with one row per (version, task, slot, item).
- `summary_overview.csv` — overall balance metrics for the chosen design.
- `summary_exposure.csv` — per-item exposure counts across versions.
- `summary_pair_counts.csv` — pairwise co-occurrence counts across all tasks.
- `design.json` — machine-readable design output for downstream tools.
- `summary.json` — machine-readable summary statistics.

In protocol mode the plugin emits `event.run.started`, optional per-version `event.run.progress` envelopes (when `verbose = true`), one `event.artifact.created` per output file, and a final `event.run.finished` (or `event.run.failed`) carrying the artifact list and elapsed time.
notes: For virtually all studies, use `objective = "sequential"` (the default). It scales to any version count, produces excellent balance, and is the easiest to tune.

For `objective = "combined"` with V > 1, you must scale both `iterations` and `cooling_rate` or the SA chain will freeze long before visiting most versions. Use `iterations = versions × 300000` and `cooling_rate ≈ 1 - 2.3 / (versions × 300000)`. Recommended lookup: 5V → 1500000 / 0.9999985; 10V → 3000000 / 0.9999992; 15V → 4500000 / 0.9999995. Do not use `combined` for V > 15.
The most reliable way to improve design quality is `n_starts = 3–5` with `threads` matched to `n_starts`, not increasing `iterations` on a single chain.
All item indices in `forbidden_pairs` are 1-based to match the human-facing CSV exports, not 0-based like the internal optimiser state.
`weight_*` parameters interact: doubling all weights leaves the optimum unchanged. Tune relative ratios, not absolute magnitudes.
`print_every` is a no-op in plugin mode and exists only for the standalone `bw-cli design` shell entry point.

see_also.description: Apply this design to raw respondent data once the survey is fielded.
Estimate utilities from coded data collected against this design.

examples.title: Basic 10-item design with 4 versions
examples.body:
[["design-maxdiff::design"]]
n_items = 10
items_per_task = 3
tasks_per_version = 8
versions = 4
run_dir = "./runs/design"
run_name = "v1"

examples.title: Larger design with multi-start parallel search
examples.body:
[["design-maxdiff::design"]]
n_items = 30
items_per_task = 5
tasks_per_version = 12
versions = 10
run_dir = "./runs/design-large"
run_name = "large_v1"
n_starts = 4
threads = 4
iterations = 500000

examples.title: Design with forbidden pairs and item labels
examples.body:
[["design-maxdiff::design"]]
n_items = 8
items_per_task = 3
tasks_per_version = 6
versions = 3
run_dir = "./runs/design-restricted"
run_name = "restricted_v1"
seed = 42
item_labels = ["red", "blue", "green", "yellow", "purple", "orange", "black", "white"]
forbidden_pairs = [[1, 2], [3, 4]]

examples.title: Combined objective with correctly scaled SA parameters (5 versions)
examples.body:
# combined is only practical for versions <= ~15 and requires scaling
# iterations and cooling_rate to compensate for the shared budget and
# continuously cooling temperature. Formula: cooling_rate ~ 1 - 2.3 / (versions * iterations)
[["design-maxdiff::design"]]
n_items = 15
items_per_task = 4
tasks_per_version = 10
versions = 5
run_dir = "./runs/design-combined"
run_name = "combined_v1"
objective = "combined"
iterations = 1500000
cooling_rate = 0.9999985
seed = 42

fields.name: n_items
fields.description: Total number of items in the study. Integer >= 1.

fields.name: items_per_task
fields.description: Items shown per task (the 'A' dimension). Integer >= 2 and <= n_items.

fields.name: tasks_per_version
fields.description: Number of tasks in each design version. Integer >= 1.

fields.name: versions
fields.description: Number of design versions to generate. Integer >= 1.

fields.name: run_dir
fields.description: Output directory. Created if it does not exist. Receives design CSVs, summary CSVs, design.json, and summary.json.

fields.name: run_name
fields.description: Short identifier for this run. Non-empty string. Tags output artifacts so multiple designs under the same run_dir do not collide.

fields.name: seed
fields.description: Random seed for reproducibility. Integer. Omit for a non-deterministic seed.

fields.name: objective
fields.description: Optimization objective. 'sequential' (default): full SA chain per version, budget applies independently per version, cross-version balance guided by global terms. 'within': same as sequential but cross-version balance terms disabled — each version optimized in isolation. 'combined': single SA chain for all versions, shared budget (~iterations/versions steps per version average); only practical for versions <= ~15 with scaled iterations and cooling_rate.

fields.name: engine
fields.description: Optimization engine. 'sa' is the simulated annealing solver (default). 'greedy' is a deterministic baseline used mainly for comparison.

fields.name: iterations
fields.description: SA iteration budget. For sequential/within: applies independently per version (total work = iterations × versions). For combined: total budget shared across all versions. Default: 300000. For combined, set iterations = versions × 300000.

fields.name: exploration_rate
fields.description: SA exploration rate: probability of a 'replace' proposal versus a 'swap' proposal at each step. Number in (0, 1]. Default: 0.65.

fields.name: initial_temperature
fields.description: SA starting temperature for the Metropolis acceptance rule. Higher values accept more uphill moves early. Number > 0. Default: 10.0. For sequential/within, temperature restarts fresh each version. For combined, cools continuously for the entire run.

fields.name: cooling_rate
fields.description: Per-iteration multiplicative cooling factor (T <- T * cooling_rate). Number in (0, 1). Default: 0.9995. With defaults, chain freezes after ~60,000 steps — fine for sequential (restarts each version) but fatal for combined with large V. For combined: cooling_rate ≈ 1 - 2.3 / (versions × iterations).

fields.name: verbose
fields.description: When true, the plugin emits one `event.run.progress` envelope per completed version (carrying the version number, best score, and accepted-move count). Defaults to false. Total envelope count scales with `versions` only - safe for any iteration count.

fields.name: print_every
fields.description: How often (in SA iterations) bw-core's internal verbose-print path emits a tick. Integer >= 1. Default: 20000. NOTE: in plugin/protocol mode this knob has no effect on the wire stream - protocol output is per-version, not per-iteration. Retained for the standalone CLI's --verbose mode.

fields.name: n_starts
fields.description: Number of independent SA restarts; the lowest-scoring design is kept. Integer >= 1. Default: 1. Values > 1 enable multi-start search and benefit from `threads`.

fields.name: threads
fields.description: Worker threads used for parallel restarts. Integer >= 1. Default: 1. Only meaningful when `n_starts` > 1.

fields.name: proposal_batch_size
fields.description: Batch size for the batched-proposals search mode (advanced). Integer >= 1. Default: 8. Ignored unless the search mode resolves to batched_proposals.

fields.name: weight_item_balance
fields.description: Sequential objective: weight on per-version item exposure balance. Number >= 0. Default: 1.0.

fields.name: weight_pair_balance
fields.description: Sequential objective: weight on per-version item-pair co-occurrence balance. Number >= 0. Default: 1.0.

fields.name: weight_global_item_balance
fields.description: Sequential objective: weight on running global item balance across versions built so far. Number >= 0. Default: 1.0.

fields.name: weight_global_pair_balance
fields.description: Sequential objective: weight on running global pair balance across versions built so far. Number >= 0. Default: 1.0.

fields.name: weight_version_item_balance
fields.description: Combined objective: weight on per-version item balance. Number >= 0. Default: 1.0.

fields.name: weight_version_pair_balance
fields.description: Combined objective: weight on per-version pair balance. Number >= 0. Default: 1.0.

fields.name: weight_total_item_balance
fields.description: Combined objective: weight on total item balance across all versions. Number >= 0. Default: 1.0.

fields.name: weight_total_pair_balance
fields.description: Combined objective: weight on total pair balance across all versions. Number >= 0. Default: 1.0.

fields.name: item_labels
fields.description: Optional human-readable labels for items. Array of strings; if provided, length must equal n_items. Used in CSV/JSON exports only - the optimizer is label-agnostic.

fields.name: forbidden_pairs
fields.description: Pairs of 1-based item indices that must never co-occur in the same task. Array of [a, b] integer pairs (each in 1..=n_items). The optimizer treats any task containing such a pair as infeasible.

artifacts.title: MaxDiff design — wide format
artifacts.description: The primary design file for survey programming. One row per (version, task) with item slot columns containing 1-based item indices. Hand this to your survey platform to programme the questionnaire.

fields.name: Version
fields.description: Design version number. Each respondent is randomly assigned one version.

fields.name: Task
fields.description: Task number within the version.

fields.name: Item1, Item2, ...
fields.description: 1-based item indices shown to the respondent in each slot of this task.

artifacts.title: Design — long format
artifacts.description: One row per (version, task, slot, item) for programmatic use by wrangling tools.

artifacts.title: Design balance summary
artifacts.description: Overall item exposure and pair-coverage statistics for the chosen design.

artifacts.title: Item exposure counts
artifacts.description: How many times each item appears across all tasks and versions. Well-balanced designs show roughly equal exposure across all items.

fields.name: item
fields.description: Item index or label.

fields.name: count
fields.description: Total appearances across all tasks and all versions.

artifacts.title: Item pair co-occurrence counts
artifacts.description: How often every unordered pair of items appears together in the same task. Balanced designs show roughly equal pair counts, ensuring that every combination of items is measured.

fields.name: item_a, item_b
fields.description: The two items in the pair.

fields.name: count
fields.description: Number of tasks where both items appeared together.
